title: Looks like somebody has been watching ep 26 too much
display_title: Looks like somebody has been watching ep 26 too much
wikitext:
[]: Looks like somebody has been watching ep. 26 too much.....             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 10:59 am http://www.perceivingreality.com/ Very interesting stuff, right there. But very Eva-ish.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Sep 25, 2010 11:01 am lol

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 11:07 am Yay, Kabbalah for EVERYBODY! Sounds like a salad.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:05 pm This kind of stuff runs through my head erryday, I don´t find it to be strange at all.

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:44 pm Hey, it actually makes a lot of sense. First time I got legitimate information of Kabbalah. Everyone I know who's heard of it is always bad mouthing it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:49 pm That's a lot to take in.

[]: I want to see where this goes.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:54 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That's a lot to take in.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:54 pm Where are you right now? In front of a computer screen?

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 1:19 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: So, this is Shinji when he grew up?

[]: Re: Looks like somebody has been watching ep. 26 too much...             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Sep 25, 2010 1:27 pm BeoX2 wrote: http://www.perceivingreality.com/ Very interesting stuff, right there. But very Eva-ish.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Sep 25, 2010 1:40 pm I can't help but wonder if this is "real" Kabbalah or the modern day LOLHOLLYWOOD Kabbalah that is oh so popular by presentation I'd say it's the latter

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 2:26 pm Xard wrote: I can't help but wonder if this is "real" Kabbalah or the modern day LOLHOLLYWOOD Kabbalah that is oh so popular by presentation I'd say it's the latter

[]: Mari doesn't take crap from no theosophists. >:[             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Sep 25, 2010 3:06 pm Xard wrote: I can't help but wonder if this is "real" Kabbalah or the modern day LOLHOLLYWOOD Kabbalah that is oh so popular by presentation I'd say it's the latter

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Sep 25, 2010 3:24 pm sure is getting new age here. It's exactly the "modern" "Kabbalah" that's all squeeee quantum mechanics desu yo ^___^

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Sep 25, 2010 5:18 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That's a lot to take in.

[]: The gist of it was "ASKADHAFGS FATHER."             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Sep 25, 2010 5:23 pm Xard wrote: sure is getting new age here. It's exactly the "modern" "Kabbalah" that's all squeeee quantum mechanics desu yo ^___^

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 25, 2010 5:23 pm BeoX2 wrote: more about the Kabbalah

[]: The clues are finally adding up.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Sep 25, 2010 5:31 pm Mr. Tines wrote: You could do worse than starting here .

[]: Re: The gist of it was "ASKADHAFGS FATHER."             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Sep 25, 2010 5:33 pm Killer Bee wrote: You obviously aren't familiar with the subject, so I'll spare you my rebuttal.

[]: Perhaps we could talk this over a spot of tea?             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Sep 25, 2010 7:10 pm Xard wrote: I'm not expert on Kabbalah but I do know far more than is worthwhile about theosophy, Kabbalah and various new age movements. Quite simply Quantum Mechanics were discovered in early 20th Century. Kabbalah is mystical Rabbinic Judaism that developed into what it is during Middle Ages. Obviously connection (or rather: parallels) between implications of QM and Kabbalah is something that has been made only during modern times. Besides, many these kind of movements "abuse" QM left and right. Kabbalah is by no means only esoteric system of thought that can be joined with modern physics. The faux-Kabbalah meshes just as well as "original" Kabbalah with these things. I must confess I didn't watch that video very long because it was starting to get too stupid. Not to mention the sleazy, clearly professionally (with big money) made nature of video doesn't exactly raise confidence. The rhethoric and presentation is exactly something Hollywood would do. I have hard time seeing any honest old time mystics doing video like that.
